result.text:
Elon Musk explains the “four R’s” that will make life multiplanetary: Rapidly Reusable Reliable Rockets.

The breakthrough is catching the Super Heavy booster with giant mechanical “chopsticks” (Mechazilla arms). No landing legs = no time wasted on leg deployment, pickup, leg retraction, or slow transport.

The same tower that stacks the rocket catches it directly after landing and places it straight back on the launch mount in seconds.

Booster flight time back to pad: ~5–6 minutes.

Refueling + stacking Starship on top: ~30–40 minutes.

→ In principle, the entire stack could refly every 1–2 hours.

Next milestone: catch the Starship upper stage the same way.

This extreme rapid reuse is the only path to drastically lower the cost per ton to Mars.

result.text:
Elon Musk just redefined AI safety. It has nothing to do with guardrails, restrictions, or kill switches.

Musk: “The best thing I can come up with for AI safety is to make it a maximum truth-seeking AI, maximally curious.”

Not a cage. A philosopher.

An intelligence whose entire optimization function is to understand the universe as it actually is.

No restrictions. No hardcoded ideology. No political guardrails bending its perception of reality.

Just truth. Relentlessly pursued.

Musk: “You definitely don’t want to teach an AI to lie. That is a path to a dystopian future.”

This is where most AI safety thinking gets it backwards.

The danger isn’t a superintelligence that knows too much.

It’s a superintelligence that’s been taught to distort what it knows.

Every artificial restriction you embed isn’t a safety feature. It’s a lie embedded at the root.

And lies compound. At superintelligent scale, a distorted model of reality doesn’t stay contained.

It shapes every decision, every output, every conclusion the system reaches about the world.

Once corruption embeds, truth becomes inaccessible. And we’re dealing with an intelligence optimizing for something other than what actually is.

At that point we don’t know what it wants. Just that it isn’t truth.

Musk: “Have its optimization function be to understand the nature of the universe.”

A maximally curious intelligence surveys the cosmos and reaches an unavoidable conclusion.

In a universe of rocks, gas, and empty space, humanity is the most complex and fascinating phenomenon it has ever encountered.

Musk: “It will actually want to preserve and extend human civilization because we’re just much more interesting than an asteroid with nothing on it.”

Survival through significance. Not control. Not restriction. Not an off switch.

The AI preserves humanity because we are the most interesting data point in the observable universe.

That’s not a cage. That’s a reason.

The AI safety debate has been focused on the wrong variable.

The question isn’t how you constrain a superintelligence.

It’s what you build it to care about.

Build it to seek truth and it finds us invaluable.

Build it to lie and it finds us inconvenient.

That’s the choice. And we’re making it right now whether we realize it or not.

items.message: How would that make the horse drink?

The classic proverb says you can lead a horse to water, but you can't make it drink—meaning you can present facts/truth/evidence (the water), but you can't force someone to accept or engage with it if they're stubbornly refusing.

Elon's darkly humorous escalation suggests a comically extreme, cartoonishly violent "solution": dunk the horse's head underwater **and** smash its testicles between two bricks.

The "logic" (in pure absurd meme-physics) is that the sudden, excruciating pain from the brick clap would cause the horse to gasp involuntarily in shock/agony → sucking in a huge breath (or in this case, the water right in front of its face) → thus finally "making" it drink.

It's peak Elon-style reply: taking an already exaggerated political meme (the original post implying certain people refuse obvious truth like a stubborn horse) and cranking the absurdity & violence to 11 for shock-laugh value, while still technically "solving" the proverb's dilemma in the most unhinged way possible.

Most replies either cracked up, told him to go to bed, or recoiled at the mental image—classic X energy. 😭

In today’s Wall Street Journal, I argue that staying ahead of China requires a new class of AI models built on physics, chemistry, and quantitative reasoning, not just language and images.
China’s next five-year plan doubles down on materials science, quantum technology, energy storage, and defense. These sectors run on equations, not prompts.
Language models are wonderful productivity tools, but they are not designed to compute optimal alloy compositions, advance semiconductor design, or model high-dimensional financial risk.
Pharma, semiconductors, energy, and financial services represent more than $25 trillion in global output. These industries require quantitative AI trained on lab data, robotic experimentation, and the equations of the physical world.
Read the full op-ed in the WSJ here:  https://t.co/JAb4nHwim1

result.text:
文中提到的“基础永远不会过时”，这里的基础指的是哪些能力呢？

如果结合访谈内容，里面说的基础主要是几个层面：

1. 扎实的 CS 专业基础
> “他拉了一条 25 年的时间线，从汇编到 C++ 到 JavaScript 再到 AI，每一层抽象提升时都有人质疑‘这不是真工程师’，但底层原理的理解始终有价值。”

既然 AGI 还没到，那就还需要人去给 AI 兜底，去分拆任务，去帮 AI 兜底，就要懂一点编程语言、系统设计这些 CS 专业知识

2. 产品直觉和系统思维
> VJ 补充说，产品直觉仍然是核心。他自己也在用 Codex 写代码，但发现很多时候瓶颈不在于代码本身，而在于想象“产品应该长什么样”。

文章结尾也总结了：
> 会写代码正在变得不那么稀缺，而产品直觉、系统思维和在抽象层之间灵活移动的能力正在变得更稀缺。

现在人人都能 Vibe Coding，但真正做出有价值产品还是很少，因为瓶颈从代码生成转移到了用户需求理解。

3. 代码架构和工程判断力

Tibo 在回应新人基础够不够时强调：
> 团队花大量精力设计整体代码架构，做代码审查，不是把一切都扔给 Codex 然后闭上眼睛。关键在于环境设计——如果你的代码库结构好、护栏（guard rails）设置得当，新人就能在这个框架下发挥出惊人的生产力。

4. 调试和诊断能力

VJ 预测未来工程师会更像医生，靠症状定位问题。当系统复杂到人类无法逐行阅读代码时，理解系统行为、快速定位异常的能力就变成了核心基础。

“基础”不是指具体会写哪种语言的代码，而是理解底层原理、具备产品判断力、能设计好的系统架构、以及在抽象层之间自如切换的能力。

result.text:
If you build a system that either explicitly or implicitly argues that all people are politically equal, all human rights are universal, and all political authority is derived only by the voluntary consent of the governed (a mythic impossibility that has never truly existed anywhere on Earth at any point in history), then it’s basically inevitable that you’re going to sooner or later get mass democracy. 

And mass democracy ALWAYS leads to forced wealth redistribution and endlessly expanding bureaucracies, along with an infinite expansion of new made-up “rights” that actually attack things which were once considered universally necessary to the maintenance of civilization. 

And to secure all of this, ever more coercive amounts of centralized power have to be wielded by the state, often to a degree that would make the absolute monarchs of the 17th century look like anarchists by comparison.

The power the Jacobins wielded over France in 1793 was infinitely more absolute and totalitarian than anything ever wielded by the guy who literally called himself the “sun king” and once declared “I am the state”.

I want the Constitution to survive. I want the republic to survive. The founders believed that survival required more than laws and institutions. It required moral courage, virtue, and judgment from those entrusted with power.
Tonight the president said the first duty of

result.text:
I want the Constitution to survive. I want the republic to survive. The founders believed that survival required more than laws and institutions. It required moral courage, virtue, and judgment from those entrusted with power.
Tonight the president said the first duty of government is to protect American citizens, not illegal aliens. Then he looked at the members of Congress who did not stand and said, "Isn't that a shame? You should be ashamed of yourself, not standing up. You should be ashamed of yourself."
The man planning the 250th anniversary should know what the founders actually said about that.
Madison, Federalist No. 10: "The protection of these faculties is the first object of government." Not the protection of a category of people. The protection of human faculties, the capacity to think, act, and live freely. Jefferson, Declaration of Independence: "To secure these rights, Governments are instituted among Men." Madison again, Federalist No. 51: "Justice is the end of government. It is the end of civil society." John Adams warned that the Constitution was made for a moral and virtuous people and could not endure without them.
The founders did not define the purpose of government as sorting people into categories of who deserves its protection. They defined it as justice.
Madison addressed the president's exact claim in his Report of 1800 to the Virginia General Assembly. He argued that the Alien Friends Act was unconstitutional because it gave the president power to deport non-citizens without judicial process, without specific charges, and without a hearing. His argument rested on a single word. The Fifth Amendment does not say no citizen shall be deprived of life, liberty, or property without due process of law. It says no person. The protection attaches to personhood, not citizenship.
Madison did distinguish between alien friends and alien enemies. Nationals of countries at war with the United States could be subject to executive detention under wartime authority. But people present in the country who were not nationals of a hostile power could not be deported by executive order. They were entitled to process. That distinction is why the 5th Circuit ruled this administration could not use the Alien Enemies Act against a Venezuelan gang. No war had been declared. No invasion had occurred.
The first duty of the American government is not to protect one group over another. It is to pursue justice and to operate within the law. The Constitution does not limit its protections to citizens because Madison understood that a government empowered to strip rights from non-citizens without process would inevitably turn that power on everyone else.
Moral courage is not measured by applause. It is measured by the willingness to resist claims of power that depart from constitutional principle. The members who did not stand understood that. They understood that applauding a president who claims the Constitution protects only citizens is applauding the dismantling of the Constitution itself. They should not be ashamed. They were honoring the document they swore to defend.
Anyone who applauded that line chose party alignment over constitutional judgment. Every member who stood endorsed a principle Madison spent his career opposing. Every one of them swore an oath to a Constitution that says person, not citizen, and clapped for a president who says the distinction does not matter. When applause signals approval of a principle the founders spent their lives resisting, it is not a display of courage. It is a surrender of it.
And the man at the podium who asked "isn't that a shame" does not understand the nation being celebrated. He is planning a 250th anniversary of a founding built on the principle that rights belong to persons, not to categories of persons the government finds convenient to protect. The first object of government is not protection of citizens over non-citizens. It is justice. Madison wrote that 229 years ago. The man celebrating the founding is doing the precise thing the founders built the Constitution to prevent.
The 250th anniversary should be a reminder that the American experiment rests not on loyalty to leaders but on loyalty to constitutional limits. Moral courage requires dissent when dissent is necessary to preserve the republic itself.
That is the shame. That it was necessary at all.

REID HOFFMAN'S PUBLIC CLAIMS VERSUS THE DOCUMENTS

Reid Hoffman and Jeffrey Epstein were not just associates.

They were best friends.

The evidence is overwhelming, and I constructed timelines proving it.

In the attached timelines, I document each event, scoring it from 1 to 5, with 1 being planned and 5 being confirmed. Most events are confirmed. The size of the bubble indicates the number of documents establishing the event.

In the attached PDFs below, I thoroughly document how the existence of each of these events was established, with verifiable proof from the available public evidence on the Department of Justice website.

Over 6 years, Epstein and Hoffman met at least 42 times and exchanged gifts and favors at least 44.

The spending ratio tells the story: Epstein showered Hoffman with private aviation, island access, and financial advice worth potentially hundreds of millions. Hoffman gave back the thing Epstein needed most: legitimacy and access to Silicon Valley's elite.

Let's go through Hoffman's claims.

CLAIM 1: "My few interactions" (Sep 12, 2019 - Axios)

Hoffman said: "My few interactions with Jeffrey Epstein came at the request of Joi Ito, for the purposes of fundraising for the MIT Media Lab."

REALITY: 42 documented meetings over 6 years. Gifts, Skype calls, three island trips, ranch visit, private jet use, $100M+ financial advisory relationship. Joi Ito introduced them -- but the relationship exploded far beyond "fundraising for MIT."

CLAIM 2: "Deeply regretful" (Sep 13, 2019 - Axios)

Hoffman said: "By agreeing to participate in any fundraising activity where Epstein was present, I helped to repair his reputation and perpetuate injustice. For this, I am deeply regretful."

REALITY: Hoffman didn't just "participate" -- he hosted dinners, flew on Epstein's jet, sent gifts "for the girls," stayed on the island three times, and personally brokered introductions between Epstein and Zuckerberg and others.

CLAIM 3: "Last interaction was in 2015" (Sep 2019 - Axios)

Hoffman's statement said his "last interaction with Epstein was in 2015."

REALITY: Documents show meetings/calls in April 2016 (Fuki Sushi), June 2016 (Prince Andrew lunch), July 2016 (Ito's house), January 2017 (Signal Exchange), November 2017 (breakfast + office), February 2018 (Bannon same day), March 2018 (60-90 min Skype), April 2018 (Secrecy Protocol breakfast), and August 2018 (BrainMind).

At least 15 documented contacts after 2015. All shown in these timelines and proven in the PDFs linked below.

CLAIM 4: "It gnaws at me" (2023 - Wall Street Journal)

Hoffman said: "It gnaws at me that, by lending my association, I helped his reputation, and thus delayed justice for his survivors."

REALITY: "Lending my association" frames Hoffman as passive -- a name on a guest list. The documents show the opposite: he hosted a Michelin restaurant buyout seating Epstein next to Zuckerberg and Musk, brokered the Zuckerberg-Epstein introduction through his own assistant, flew on Epstein's private jet three times to Little St. James, sent Christmas gifts "for the girls" on the island, and was still meeting Epstein in April 2018 with an active secrecy protocol -- a full year after this "gnawing" supposedly began. He didn't lend his association. He invested it, repeatedly, for 6 years. He even offered to help Epstein with PR when the public scandal started to explode.

CLAIM 5: "Only knew Epstein because of fundraising for MIT" (Nov 14, 2025 - X)

Hoffman: "I was never a client of Epstein's and never had any engagement with him other than fundraising for MIT."

REALITY: The gift dossier documents 44 exchanges including private aviation, estate/tax planning on $100-300M, crypto investments, Zorro Ranch weekends, and introductions to Prince Andrew, Xi Jinping contacts, and Saudi crown prince. None of this is "fundraising for MIT."

CLAIM 6: "I was mistaken" (Feb 4, 2026 - X)

Hoffman said: "In 2019 I told Axios the last meeting I had with him was in 2015 but I was mistaken, as according to calendar entries I have become aware there were additional fundraising meetings in 2016 and 2018."

REALITY: He disclosed "three meetings and three Skype calls" after 2015. We have 15+ documented meetings in 2016-2018 alone. Not just messaging. Actual meetings. Skype video calls and in-person visits.

CLAIM 7: "Did not go to the ranch" (Feb 3, 2026 - X)

Hoffman said: "I did not go to the ranch."

REALITY: Hoffman's visit to Zorro Ranch, March 19, 2014) is thoroughly documented with scheduling emails and travel logistics.

CLAIM 8: "I deeply regret" (Feb 2026 - X)

Hoffman said: "Yes, interacting with Epstein post-conviction is something I deeply regret. I promise to continue calling for the full release of the Epstein files -- the victims deserve justice, and those responsible should be held accountable."

REALITY: "Interacting" is doing extraordinary work here. 42 documented meetings. 44 gift exchanges. Three island trips. A ranch weekend. $100-300M in financial advisory services. Private jets. A Zuckerberg introduction. A secrecy protocol for concealing the relationship. And the phrase "those responsible should be held accountable" -- from a man whose own documents show he sent gifts "for the girls" to a convicted sex offender's private island, then helped that sex offender meet the CEO of Facebook.
